subtasks.title: Write unit tests for Bearer token extraction (REQ-2)

TestMiddleware_InvalidAuthFormat: 'Basic ...' scheme → HTTP 401 'invalid authorization header format'.

TestMiddleware_WellFormedHeader: 'Bearer <token>' proceeds past extraction (no extraction error).

TestParseToken_EmptyString: empty string → non-nil error.
TestParseToken_WrongPrefix: wrong prefix → non-nil error.

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for hashToken and constant-time comparison (REQ-11)

TestHashToken_EmptyString: hashToken('') returns valid 64-char hex.

task_groups.title: Implement Permission Registry and Context Types

subtasks.title: Implement PermissionRegistry with built-in permissions
details: Create internal/auth/permissions.go.
Define PermissionRegistry struct with a sync.RWMutex and map[string]struct{} for registered permissions.
Implement NewPermissionRegistry() pre-populating users:read, orgs:read, keys:read, keys:manage, tokens:read, tokens:manage.
Implement Register(resourceType, action string) error: validate non-empty, lowercase letters/digits/underscores only; return error on duplicate; add to map under rwmutex write lock.
Implement IsValid(resourceType, action string) bool: lookup under read lock.
Implement List() []string: collect all keys, sort ascending, return under read lock.
Validate resource_type and action with regexp `^[a-z0-9_]+$`.

subtasks.title: Implement AuthInfo struct and request context helpers
details: Create internal/auth/context.go.
Define unexported type `type contextKey string` and `const authInfoKey contextKey = "auth_info"`.
Define AuthInfo struct with fields: CredentialType, UserID, Role, KeyID, TokenID string; Permissions []string.
Implement GetAuthInfo(c echo.Context) *AuthInfo: retrieve from context using authInfoKey, type-assert, return nil if absent.
Implement GetUserID(c echo.Context) string: call GetAuthInfo; return UserID or empty string if nil.
Implement IsAdmin(c echo.Context) bool: return true if AuthInfo non-nil and (Role=="admin" || CredentialType=="admin_token").
Store AuthInfo in context via c.Set(string(authInfoKey), &authInfo).

subtasks.title: Implement hashToken utility function
details: In internal/auth/auth.go (or a dedicated util file), implement hashToken(input string) string.
Use sha256.Sum256([]byte(input)) and hex.EncodeToString(hash[:]) to produce a 64-char lowercase hex string.
Function is package-private (unexported); used by all credential validation functions.
Document that both computed and stored hashes must be converted to []byte before subtle.ConstantTimeCompare.

task_groups.title: Implement Token Parsing and Extraction Logic

subtasks.title: Implement parseToken function with detection-order precedence
details: Create/extend internal/auth/auth.go with parseToken(token string) (credType string, components []string, err error).
Use apikit.TokenPrefix as the prefix variable for all pattern matching.
Check admin pattern first: strings.HasPrefix(token, prefix+"_admin_"); validate hex suffix is exactly 64 chars using hex.DecodeString.
Check PAT pattern second: strings.HasPrefix(token, prefix+"_pat_"); split remainder on '_' expecting exactly 2 parts.
Fall back to API key: split token after prefix+'_' on '_' expecting exactly 2 parts (key_id, secret).
Return error with descriptive internal message for unrecognized format; caller maps to HTTP 401 'unrecognized token format'.

subtasks.title: Implement Bearer token extraction in middleware entry point
details: In NewAuthMiddleware's returned MiddlewareFunc, read c.Request().Header.Get("Authorization").
If empty → return APIError(401, "missing authorization header").
If not strings.HasPrefix(header, "Bearer ") → return APIError(401, "invalid authorization header format").
Extract token = header[len("Bearer "):].
If token == "" → return APIError(401, "missing token").
Pass token to parseToken; if error → return APIError(401, "unrecognized token format").
Dispatch to credential-type-specific validation based on credType.

checks: Extraction and parsing unit tests pass: go test ./internal/auth/... -run 'TestParseToken|TestMiddleware_Missing|TestMiddleware_Invalid|TestMiddleware_Empty|TestMiddleware_Bearer|TestMiddleware_Unrecognized' -count=1 -v
Detection order property test passes: go test ./internal/auth/... -run 'TestProperty_DetectionOrder' -count=1 -v
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/auth/...
Requirements 05-REQ-2.1–05-REQ-2.4, 05-REQ-3.1–05-REQ-3.5 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Implement Admin Token and API Key Credential Validation

subtasks.title: Implement admin token validation with SHA-256 hash and constant-time comparison
details: Create internal/auth/credentials.go with validateAdminToken(ctx context.Context, db *db.DB, fullToken string) (*AuthInfo, error).
Validate hex suffix is exactly 64 chars: extract suffix after '<prefix>_admin_', call hex.DecodeString; if fails/wrong length → return 401 'invalid credentials' without DB query.
Compute hash = hashToken(fullToken) (hashes full token string including prefix).
Query admin_config table for key='admin_token_hash'; if db.ErrNotFound → return 401 'invalid credentials'; other error → log and return 500 'internal server error'.
Use subtle.ConstantTimeCompare([]byte(hash), []byte(storedHash)); if != 1 → return 401 'invalid credentials'.
On success, return &AuthInfo{CredentialType: "admin_token", UserID: "", Role: "admin"}.

subtasks.title: Implement API key validation with lookup, expiry, revocation, and user status checks
details: Implement validateAPIKey(ctx context.Context, db *db.DB, keyID, secret string) (*AuthInfo, error).
Query api_keys by key_id; if db.ErrNotFound → 401 'invalid credentials'; other error → 500.
Check revoked_at: if non-NULL → 401 'credential revoked'.
Check expires_at: if non-NULL and time.Now().After(expiresAt) → 401 'credential expired'.
Use subtle.ConstantTimeCompare([]byte(hashToken(secret)), []byte(row.SecretHash)); if != 1 → 401 'invalid credentials'.
Query users by user_id; if error → 500; if status=='blocked' → 403 'user is blocked'.
Return &AuthInfo{CredentialType: "api_key", UserID: user.ID, Role: user.Role, KeyID: keyID}.
Use db.ParseTime for expires_at/revoked_at timestamp fields.

checks: Admin token validation tests pass: go test ./internal/auth/... -run 'TestMiddleware_.*Admin' -count=1 -v
API key validation tests pass: go test ./internal/auth/... -run 'TestMiddleware_.*APIKey|TestMiddleware_Blocked.*API' -count=1 -v
Constant-time comparison property test passes: go test ./internal/auth/... -run 'TestProperty_ConstantTime' -count=1 -v
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/auth/...
Requirements 05-REQ-4.1–05-REQ-4.5, 05-REQ-5.1–05-REQ-5.6 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Implement PAT Validation and RBAC Authorization Helpers

subtasks.title: Implement PAT validation with permissions injection
details: Implement validatePAT(ctx context.Context, db *db.DB, tokenID, secret string) (*AuthInfo, error) in credentials.go.
Query pats by token_id; if db.ErrNotFound → 401 'invalid credentials'; other error → 500.
Check revoked_at: if non-NULL → 401 'credential revoked'.
Check expires_at: if non-NULL and past → 401 'credential expired'.
Use subtle.ConstantTimeCompare([]byte(hashToken(secret)), []byte(row.SecretHash)); if != 1 → 401 'invalid credentials'.
Query users by user_id; if error → 500; if status=='blocked' → 403 'user is blocked'.
Deserialize permissions JSON array from pats row into []string.
Return &AuthInfo{CredentialType: "pat", UserID: user.ID, Role: user.Role, TokenID: tokenID, Permissions: permissions}.

subtasks.title: Implement RequireAdmin, RequireOwnerOrAdmin, and RequirePermission RBAC helpers
details: Implement RequireAdmin(c echo.Context) error: call IsAdmin(c); if false → return APIError(403, "forbidden").
Implement RequireOwnerOrAdmin(c echo.Context, resourceOwnerID string) error: call GetUserID(c); if matches resourceOwnerID → nil; else call RequireAdmin(c).

Implement RequirePermission(c echo.Context, resourceType, action string) error: get AuthInfo; if nil or CredentialType != 'pat' → return nil (bypass); iterate Permissions for 'resourceType:action'; if found → nil; else → APIError(403, "insufficient permissions").
Handle nil AuthInfo in RequireAdmin: return APIError(403, "forbidden") (per 05-REQ-7.E1).
Handle empty resourceOwnerID in RequireOwnerOrAdmin: empty string != any valid UUID → falls to admin check (per 05-REQ-7.E2).

RBAC helper tests pass: go test ./internal/auth/... -run 'TestRequireAdmin|TestRequireOwner|TestRequirePermission' -count=1 -v
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/auth/...

task_groups.title: Implement NewAuthMiddleware Constructor and Full Integration

subtasks.title: Implement NewAuthMiddleware constructor with nil guard and dispatch logic
details: In internal/auth/auth.go, implement NewAuthMiddleware(database *db.DB, registry *PermissionRegistry) echo.MiddlewareFunc.
Panic immediately with descriptive message if database == nil or registry == nil.
Return an echo.MiddlewareFunc that: extracts token, calls parseToken, dispatches to validateAdminToken/validateAPIKey/validatePAT based on credType.
On successful validation, call c.Set(string(authInfoKey), authInfo) then return next(c).
On any validation error, return the appropriate APIError response without calling next.
All responses use APIError() helper from Server Core for consistent JSON envelope.

subtasks.title: Implement blocked user enforcement ordering in middleware dispatch
details: Ensure blocked user check occurs inside validateAPIKey and validatePAT AFTER secret hash comparison passes.
Admin token path does not query users table at all.
When user blocked: return immediately with APIError(403, "user is blocked") without calling next or setting AuthInfo in context.
Verify ordering: invalid secret for blocked user → 401 'invalid credentials' (not 403), because hash check runs before user lookup.

subtasks.title: Wire middleware into APIGroup and verify unprotected path exclusion
details: Register NewAuthMiddleware on the Echo APIGroup (not the root router).
Confirm /healthz, /readyz, /version, /auth/providers, /auth/callback are on a separate unprotected group or root router.
All error responses from middleware use APIError() with exactly the 11 approved message strings.
Confirm Content-Type: application/json on all error responses.
Document wiring location in code comment: 'middleware applied to APIGroup, not root router'.

task_groups.title: Wiring Verification: End-to-End Integration and Stub Audit

subtasks.title: Trace all execution paths and verify function chain integrity
details: PATH-1 (API key): NewAuthMiddleware → extractToken → parseToken → validateAPIKey → hashToken → subtle.ConstantTimeCompare → user lookup → c.Set(authInfoKey) → next(c). Verify each step calls the next without bypassing.
PATH-2 (PAT): same chain through validatePAT, then handler calls RequirePermission → checks Permissions slice.
PATH-3 (admin token): parseToken → validateAdminToken → hashToken(fullToken) → admin_config query → subtle.ConstantTimeCompare → c.Set → next(c).
PATH-4 (missing header): extractToken → APIError(401) → return (next never called).
PATH-5 (blocked user): validateAPIKey/validatePAT → secret hash OK → user blocked → APIError(403) → return.
PATH-6 (custom permission): registry.Register → NewAuthMiddleware → validatePAT → AuthInfo.Permissions → RequirePermission finds permission → nil.
Confirm return values from each function are consumed by the caller (no dropped errors).

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation and no dropped errors
details: Review internal/auth/credentials.go: every db query error is checked; non-ErrNotFound errors are logged and returned as HTTP 500.
Review internal/auth/auth.go: the return value of validateAdminToken, validateAPIKey, validatePAT is always checked before calling next(c).
RequireAdmin, RequireOwnerOrAdmin, RequirePermission: callers in handlers must check and return the error.
Use 'go vet' and errcheck (if available) to confirm no error return values are silently discarded.
Confirm that the test for DB error paths (TS-05-E6, TS-05-E8, TS-05-E9, TS-05-E11, TS-05-E12) all pass with real middleware.

subtasks.title: Run all smoke tests with real components
details: Run: go test ./internal/auth/... -run 'TestSMOKE_' -count=1 -v
All 6 smoke tests pass: API key auth, PAT with permission, admin token, missing header, blocked user, custom permission.
Each smoke test uses real Echo router, real SQLite db, real auth middleware — no mocked middleware.
Confirm handler invocation tracking works correctly (handler called/not called as expected).
Confirm AuthInfo fields are correctly populated in all success paths.

subtasks.title: Stub and dead-code audit across all auth package files
details: Run: grep -rn 'panic("not implemented")' internal/auth/*.go — must return no results in non-test files.
Run: grep -rn 'TODO\|FIXME\|HACK' internal/auth/*.go — review and resolve any found.
Confirm no bare 'return nil' in non-trivial error paths (e.g., after a DB call that could fail).
Confirm all five credential validation steps are fully implemented: extraction, parsing, admin validation, API key validation, PAT validation.
Verify subtle.ConstantTimeCompare is present in credentials.go at least 3 times (one per credential type).

subtasks.title: Cross-spec entry point verification with server_core and database_layer
details: Verify APIError() from spec 01 (server_core) is called correctly: signature matches and produces {"error":{"code":N,"message":M}} JSON.
Verify apikit.TokenPrefix from spec 01 is read at parseToken call time (not hardcoded 'ak').
